assumptions: The exposure in question is a foreign currency exposure arising in consolidated financial statements.
The issue is whether that exposure could be designated within an IFRS hedge accounting relationship.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRS 9 identify fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRS 9 identify cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposures to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39, IFRS 9, IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting model for foreign currency monetary items and exchange differences outside hedge accounting.
